q: What is the explicit expression for the time-evolution operator U(t) when the Hamiltonian H is completely time-independent?
gold: U(t) = exp(-iHt/hbar)

q: State the mathematical condition that a linear operator A must satisfy to be Hermitian.
gold: <phi|A psi> = <A phi|psi> for all states, i.e. A = A-dagger

q: What are the eigenvalues of the parity operator P, and what property do its eigenstates have?
gold: Eigenvalues are +1 and -1; eigenstates have definite parity (even/symmetric or odd/antisymmetric)

q: Write the fundamental commutation relation between position x and momentum p.
gold: [x, p] = i hbar

q: By what factor does the energy of the first excited state (n=1) of a 1D harmonic oscillator exceed its ground-state (n=0) energy?
gold: By a factor of 3 (E1=3/2 hbar omega, E0=1/2 hbar omega)

q: How is the total probability of finding a particle anywhere in space related to the normalization integral of its wavefunction?
gold: It equals 1: integral of |psi|^2 over all space = 1

q: What is the mathematical definition of the expectation value <A> of an operator A in a normalized state |psi>?
gold: <A> = <psi|A|psi>

q: If two operators A and B commute ([A,B]=0), what property do their eigenfunctions share?
gold: They share a complete set of simultaneous (common) eigenfunctions

q: What is the commutator [Lx, Ly] of the components of orbital angular momentum?
gold: [Lx, Ly] = i hbar Lz

q: Write the time-dependent Schrodinger equation for a particle of mass m in a potential V(r,t).
gold: i hbar d psi/dt = [ -hbar^2/2m laplacian + V(r,t) ] psi

q: What constraint does the Pauli exclusion principle place on the total wavefunction of identical fermions?
gold: The total wavefunction must be completely antisymmetric under exchange of any two identical fermions

q: What is the physical interpretation of |psi(r)|^2 according to the Born rule?
gold: It is the probability density of finding the particle at position r

q: What are the energy eigenvalues E_n of a particle of mass m in an infinite 1D square well of width a?
State the value of the total spin angular momentum squared S^2 for an electron in terms of hbar.
What defines the lowering (annihilation) operator A of a harmonic oscillator in terms of x and p?
gold: A = sqrt(m omega/2 hbar) (x + i p/(m omega))

q: Under what condition is the probability density |psi(r,t)|^2 entirely independent of time?
gold: When the particle is in a stationary state (eigenstate of the time-independent Hamiltonian)

q: What are the allowed values of the magnetic quantum number m for a given orbital angular momentum quantum number l?
What property must the eigenvalues of a Hermitian operator possess?
gold: They must be real numbers

q: How does the transmission coefficient T behave when a particle's energy E is less than the barrier height V0?
gold: T > 0: nonzero, exponentially small probability of tunneling through the barrier

q: What values of the total angular momentum quantum number j arise from coupling orbital l with spin s=1/2?
gold: j = l + 1/2 and j = l - 1/2 (for l>0; j=1/2 if l=0)
